name: New Jersey Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to New Jersey - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: State Board of Dietetics and Nutrition
provider.parentOrganization: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs

address.description:
Division of Consumer Affairs
PO Box 45021
Newark, NJ 07101

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: Active CDR registration as an RD/RDN will be the primary qualifying credential.

requiredDocuments.name: Application Form (Pending)
requiredDocuments.description: Application materials will be released by the Board once regulations are finalized.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: Is New Jersey currently issuing dietitian licenses?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Although NJ enacted a licensure law in 2020, the Board of Dietetics and Nutrition is still drafting the implementing regulations. Licenses will be issued once these regulations are officially adopted.

mainEntity.name: When will New Jersey start accepting license applications?

acceptedAnswer.text: No firm date has been announced. The Board is holding public sessions to finalize regulations. Monitor the NJ Division of Consumer Affairs website for updates.

mainEntity.name: Can I practice as a dietitian in New Jersey now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Until the regulations are fully enacted, practicing with a CDR credential (RD/RDN) in New Jersey remains the standard. Employers generally require active CDR registration.

mainEntity.name: Does New Jersey have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in New Jersey. To work in New Jersey, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to New Jersey?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take TBD (Regulations Pending).

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in New Jersey while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
